text: Maximal strength training combined with endurance training improves running economy at marathon pace in master marathon runners.
Maximal strength training concurrent with endurance training significantly increases 1 repetition maximum in master endurance runners.
General resistance training (non-maximal) combined with endurance training does not significantly improve running economy or strength in master marathon runners.
Concurrent strength and endurance training does not alter body composition or anthropometric measures in master marathon runners.
Rate of force development may be the key mechanism through which maximal strength training improves running economy, making heavy/maximal loading more effective than moderate resistance training.
